title: Money & Finance
objective: Students will explore money & finance concepts including Budgeting Pocket Money, Making Change, Advertising & Spending and more.

steps.content: Welcome to an exciting lesson about Budgeting Pocket Money!
What a budget is; planning how to spend a fixed amount of pocket money or allowance; making trade-offs between different things you want to buy
Time to try this out: Create a simple spending plan for £10 showing what they would buy and how much is left
If you had £10 to spend at a school fair, could they plan in advance what to spend it on so the money lasts?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Budgeting Pocket Money. Create a simple spending plan for £10 showing what they would buy and how much is left Explain what a budget is and why it helps to plan spending
Today we're going to learn about Making Change. Get ready for an adventure!
Calculating change from a purchase; working confidently with pounds and pence together; solving practical money problems involving addition and subtraction
Let me show you something cool. Calculate the change from £5 when buying items costing pounds and pence
If you bought a comic for £2.75 and paid with a £5 note, could they work out they should get £2.25 back?
Great job! Today we learned about Making Change. Calculate the change from £5 when buying items costing pounds and pence Add two or three prices together to find a total cost
Have you ever thought about Advertising & Spending? Let's find out together!
How advertising tries to influence what we buy; being a critical consumer; understanding 'value for money'; the difference between emotional and rational spending
Time to try this out: Spot at least two persuasion techniques in a real advert (bright colours, celebrity, special offer)
When you sees an advert for a toy or game, can they recognise that the advert is trying to make them want to buy it?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Advertising & Spending. Spot at least two persuasion techniques in a real advert (bright colours, celebrity, special offer) Explain what 'value for money' means and compare two similar products at different prices
Let's explore Banks & Saving! Have you ever wondered about this?
What banks do and why people use them; bank accounts as safe places for money; that savings in a bank can earn interest; children's savings accounts
Time to try this out: Explain what a bank does in simple terms (keeps money safe, lets you save)
Could you explain what a bank does and why people put their money there instead of keeping it all at home?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Banks & Saving. Explain what a bank does in simple terms (keeps money safe, lets you save) Describe what interest means (the bank pays you a little extra for keeping money there)
Today we're going to learn about Fair Trade & Ethics. Get ready for an adventure!
Where products come from and who makes them; that people around the world produce what we buy; fair pay for workers; making ethical choices as consumers
Let's practice together! Explain that the things we buy are made by real people, sometimes in other countries
If you saw a Fairtrade logo on a chocolate bar, could they explain what it means and why it matters?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Fair Trade & Ethics. Explain that the things we buy are made by real people, sometimes in other countries Describe what 'fair trade' means in simple terms (workers get paid fairly)
Have you ever thought about Ways to Pay? Let's find out together!
Different ways to pay: cash, debit cards, contactless, online payments, mobile payments; that digital payments still use real money; keeping payment details safe
Time to try this out: Name at least four different ways people can pay for things
Does you understand that when you tap a card or use a phone to pay, real money leaves your bank account even though you can't see coins changing hands?
You did it! Here's what we explored about Ways to Pay: Name at least four different ways people can pay for things Explain that tapping a card or phone still spends real money from a bank account
Let's discover the amazing world of Earning Money!
Ways that children and adults earn money; the connection between work, skills and pay; that harder or more skilled work often pays more
Time to try this out: Name at least three different ways people can earn money
Could you explain why some jobs pay more than others and describe a way they might earn some money themselves?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Earning Money. Name at least three different ways people can earn money Explain why a doctor might earn more than a shop assistant (training, skills, responsibility)
